description: Perform a detailed Under-Keel Clearance (UKC) calculation for the VLCC 'MT Pacific Voyager' entering Southampton using the tidal, vessel, and channel data provided in /home/ga/Documents/PassagePlanning/. Calculate the Net UKC for every half-hour interval [Net UKC = (Charted Depth + Tide) - (Draft + Squat + Swell Allowance); Squat(m) = Cb * V^2 / 100] to identify safe transit windows of at least 2 hours where Net UKC is at least 1.0m. Select the optimal window with the highest safety margin and document the results in a Passage Plan at /home/ga/Documents/PassagePlanning/ukc_passage_plan.txt, including the calculated Squat value, minimum required Tidal Height, a list of safe windows, and justification for the chosen window. Additionally, create a Bridge Command scenario in /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/p) Southampton Deep Draft Transit/ by configuring environment.ini (Setting='Solent', Month=11, StartTime 1 hour before the optimal window), ownship.ini (Name='MT Pacific Voyager', Speed=6.0, Latitude 50.75-50.85, Longitude -1.40 to -1.20), and othership.ini (minimum 2 traffic vessels). Update the bc5.ini configuration to enable Full Radar with a Max Range of 48 nm and Range Resolution of 128 cells.
